entry.title: MISSING MANDATE REFERENCE
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have identified that the "Mandate Reference" field is populated in the accounting document when it is created through sales integration. However, when the same document is posted directly in Finance, the field remains empty/blank.

This behaviour is highly critical as it prevents the grouping of open items when issuing SEPA Direct Debits.

We encountered this issue during our first SDD run and identified the root cause based on OSS Note 3418697
Please let us know if you need any further details.

Best regards,
Davide

entry.title: Can Variant Management be used to switch between List View and Reporting View with the same data?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently working on a Fiori application where I need to present the same underlying data in two different views: a classic List View and a Reporting View (for example, an OverviewPage). My question is:

Is it possible to use Variant Management to switch between these two distinct app views?

So far, I've used Variant Management primarily for storing and restoring filters, columns, or layout settings within a single template. However, my idea is to see if I can leverage it to swap out entire templates (i.e., switching from a List View to a Reporting View) while using the same data source.

If anyone has experience with this scenario or can share insights on whether this is feasible—or if not, what alternative approaches (such as routing or cross-application navigation) might be recommended—I would greatly appreciate your input.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: Failed to load UI5 component for navigation intent, f...
entry.content:
tbh I don't know much about changing the SAPUI5-Version for BTP deployment. I don't think that you can change the SAPUI5-Version in the manifest.json, at least there is the "minUI5Version" under sap.ui5/dependencies but according to the documentation it is just for information:
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/be0cf40f61184b358b5faedaec98b2da

You might need to check if you can set a different version for the BTP launchpad itself, or you need to adjust your app to work with the current version there (1.120.x is the latest long-term version, maybe it is the "newest" one available in this case).

entry.title: Re: Negative Balance Restriction based on future accruals.
Re: MD51 not work for project by MRP area
entry.content:
Hello

Check the information below from note 550844:

3. Can I use the make-to-order production or engineer-to-order planning strategies in MRP areas?
Answer:
The strategies of the make-to-order planning can be used only in the plant MRP area. Therefore, individual customer segments are always in the plant MRP area even if a storage location that refers to a storage location MRP area is assigned in the sales order. You cannot plan individual customer segments in the MRP area.
As of SAP Enhancement Package 3 for SAP ERP 6.0, you can use the business function OPS_PS_CI_1 in the project system to achieve a work breakdown structure (WBS) account assignment on MRP area level. Restriction: Note that to procure project account-assigned requirements from MRP, you can only create purchase requisitions or stock transfer requisitions. For technical reasons, you cannot use stock transfer reservations for the procurement.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: How to bind sap.fe.macros table to data from association?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I hope this time anyone answers my question... 

I'm working on a FE app using Flexible Programming Model and OData V4. I'm trying to call a dialog upon pressing an action button on the object page. The dialog should contain a macros table with the data that comes from the root entity's association. Unfortunately, whatever I try, it doesn't display any data. Here's the working example where my dialog displays the root entity's data (which isn't what I want to achieve):

<macros:Table id="changeHistoryDialogTable"
metaPath="/BatchRelease/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem"
readOnly="true" />

 

But I want something like this:
 
<macros:Table id="changeHistoryDialogTable"
metaPath="/BatchRelease/_ChangeHistory/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem"
readOnly="true" />​
 
And it doesn't work. I tried passing contextPath, all possible combinations in the metaPath field - all in vain.
 
My Fiori version is 1.108.37 (S/4HANA On-Prem 2022)
 
I'd appreciate any suggestions.
Regrads,
Mykyta

entry.title: Fiori disable create dialog for UUID field using Fiori OData v4 with draft
entry.content:
Hi Devs,

do you know if there is a setting what we can apply for RAP backends to suppress the Create Dialog?

Our scenario is OData v4 draft enabled based on RAP.
Unfortunately beside the managed numbering mentioned here we have other UUID fields (foreign keys) which result in showing the dialog, but the goal is to suppress it.

In OData V2 apps w/o draft the behavior is the opposite, you need to explicitly specify in manifest.json the setting "createWithParameterDialog" to have a similar dialog.

Workaround I want to eliminate, is that we do hack into the Fiori Elements template events from extensions, to hook our custom handlers. This way we can react on dialog open, to be able to fill fields (context) in the background of the empty new draft entity instance being created.

Otherwise context dependent value helps with additional binding defined in ABAP CDS does not work.

Custom dialogs we can do of course, but there is maybe a magic which works, just simply I missed to identify it.
Thank You for any advise, which is not an unstable layout dependent workaround like the above  😏 !
BR,Attila

entry.title: Configurando certificado propio SSL para SAP Business One 10 FP 2305
entry.content:
Problema:

Importar un certificado SSL de dominio propio (comprado con un tercero)

Solución:

La solución es en 2 partes:

1.-Debe actualizarse el certificado para SLD (puerto 40000)

2.-Debe actualizarse el certificado del servidor de autenticación (puerto 40020)

PASO 1

Para el puerto del SLD

Se debe acceder al servidor Linux e ingresar a la siguiente carpeta:

/usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/ServerTools/SLD/tools

en esta misma carpeta se debe poner el certificado con extensión .p12 (para convertirlo en este formato, necesitas tener el .cer y .key y procesarlo desde OpenSSL)

Posteriormente debe de ejecutarse el comando:

sh update_https_certificate.sh

y deberán seguirse los pasos de la nota 2046101: (https://me.sap.com/notes/2046101/E)

  1. Log on to your Linux server and navigate to the folder <Installation Path>/SAPBusinessOne/ServerTools/SLD/tools/.
  2. Execute the shell script update_https_certificate.sh in a command-line terminal.
  3. In the terminal, enter the following information step by step:
    1. Path to the Tomcat server for the SLD (<Installation Path>/SAPBusinessOne/Common/tomcat).
    2. Path to the new certificate, e.g. <Certificate Path>/server.p12.
    3. Password for the new certificate.

la ruta de tomcat es:

/usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/tomcat

Una vez realizados los pasos anteriores debe reiniciarse el servertools con el comando:

systemctl restart sapb1servertools

luego validamosque el servicio haya iniciado correctamente con : 

systemctl status sapb1servertools

deberá verse de la siguiente manera:

Para validar que el cambio haya sido aplicado, desde el servidor de windows ingresa al sitio:

https://<tudominio.com>:40000

(asegurate de cambiar tudominio.com por el de tu certificado)

PASO 2

Actualizando el certificado para el servidor de autenticación puerto 40020

Para este caso es necesario hacer una reconfiguración del Component Server Tools en HANA:

 

Para esto debes seguir los pasos del enlace siguiente:

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE_ADMIN_GUIDE_HANA/1a2fc202f7f64336abf9fbc957d9b9ba/af036ddec6634450ab752fa98f4f59fb.html

En especifico los pasos del 1 al 4:

Log in to the Linux server as root.

In a command line terminal, navigate to the installation folder of SAP Business One where the setup script is located. By default the installation folder is /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne.

Enter the following command: ./setup.

The server components setup wizard is launched.

In the welcome page, select Reconfiguration and choose Next.

No se deberá cambiar ninguna configuración, solamente el paso que debe modificarse es cuando solicita el certificado de seguridad:

Tambien se puede revisar información adicional en el siguiente enlace:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE_IAM/548d6202b2b6491b824a488cfc447343/0566e7674c924df3b8f0ff301d2fa098.html?version=10.0_FP_2208

Al finalizar, se deben de reiniciar los servicios siguientes:

systemctl restart sapb1servertools
service sapb1servertools-authentication restart

deben de ser validados:

systemctl status sapb1servertools
service sapb1servertools-authentication status

Si todo sale correcto se puede validar el certificado del puerto 40020 en la siguiente liga:

https://tudominio.com:40020/auth/

Se debe de ver de la siguiente manera y tambien revisar la vigencia del certificado, siguiendo los pasos que previamente indicamos para el puerto 40000

Para finalizar puedes verificar que los servicios sean accesibles desde el exterior, ya sea el web client o las app B1 Sales o B1 Service

Espero les ayude a despejar algunas dudas.

entry.title: Prevent Costly Disruptions: Mitigating Data Discrepancy Risks in S/4HANA
entry.content:
In today's data-driven business landscapes, testing with accurate and consistent data across enterprise systems is paramount. Testing with inconsistent data can lead to significant challenges. Understanding the real-world implications of these discrepancies and implementing proactive measures can save organizations substantial time, resources, and effort.

Real-Life incidents because of data discrepancies:

  1. Unity Technologies' $110 Million Advertisement Targeting Error

In Q1 2022, Unity Technologies, renowned for its real-time 3D content platform, faced a substantial data quality issue. Their Audience Pinpoint tool ingested erroneous data from a major customer, leading to inaccuracies in their machine learning models for advertisement targeting. This mishap resulted in $110 million revenue loss, encompassing direct financial impact, costs associated with model retraining, and delays in launching new features.

  1. Equifax's Inaccurate Credit Score Reporting

Between March and April 2022, Equifax, a leading credit reporting agency, provided incorrect credit scores to major lenders, affecting millions of consumers. For over 300,000 individuals, scores deviated by 20 points or more, leading to unfavorable loan terms or outright denials. This incident not only undermined consumer trust but also exposed Equifax to potential legal and financial repercussions.

  1. Uber's $45 Million Driver Payment Miscalculation

Uber experienced a significant data discrepancy that led to its drivers underpaying by approximately $45 million. The error stemmed from a miscalculation in driver commissions, highlighting the profound financial and reputational impact that data inaccuracies can have on a company's operations.

Test Data Express seamlessly transfers production data into Quality and Development environments for unit testing. This helps reduce production issues and response times. As an end result it improves efficiency, saves costs, and allows business and support teams to focus on higher-value tasks.

Forestallers offers monthly or quarterly Data Transfers to landscapes at a minimum cost.

Please email(request4demo@forestallers.com) or give us a call to schedule the Demo and get your questions answered

#testdataexpress #forestallers @testdatarefresh #BDC

entry.title: SAP 公有云替代料的使用
entry.content:
SAP中的替代物料有几种，比如生产订单中A物料用完之后系统自动切换到B物料（使用物料主数据MRP4视图中的 “后继物料” 字段），或者在BOM中设置主物料和替代物料的优先级，当主物料库存够，可用性检查取主物料，主物料库存不够但替代料库存够，可用性检查取替代料；或者 主物料用80% 替代物料用20% （使用BOM中的固定比例）。

本文主要介绍下替代料在BOM中的使用，

在创建或更改BOM时，在对应的组件行项中创建 “替代项目组”，将主料和替代料设在一个“替代项目组”中。

主料设置：

优先级设为：1     策略选择：1     使用率：100%

替代料设置：

优先级设为：2     策略选择：1     使用率：空

在工单中进行 “可用性检查”时，一般根据 订单数量，主料库存数量 和 替代料库存数量决定选择。

  1. 当主料库存数量 > 替代料库存数量，可用性检查时系统都会自动选择主料。
  2. 当主料库存数量 < 替代料库存数量时：

a.主料库存数量 >= 订单数量时，可用性检查时，系统会自动选择主料。

举例：订单数量是100， 主料库存120，替代料库存是150.

               可用性检查会选择 主料

b. 主料库存数量< 订单数量，且替代料库存数量 > = 订单数量时，系统会自动选择替代料。

举例：订单数量150， 主料库存 120， 替代料库存150.

这时可用性检查发现 主料库存无法满足订单需求，替代料库存满足订单需求，系统就会选择替代料。

c. 主料库存数量< 订单数量，且替代料库存数量 <订单数量时,系统会自动选择主料。

举例：订单数量 200，主料库存120， 替代料库存150.

这时可用性检查发现 主料和替代料的库存都不满足需求，系统会自动选择主料。这块因为前面的BOM的设置，系统只会在 主料和替代料中选择一个。

如果想 主料和替代料的库存都被用到，那可以调整BOM中的设置，设置固定的比例，那系统会按固定比例在订单中 分配 主料和辅料的使用量。

主料BOM设置：

替代料设置：

举例说明：

BOM设置： 主料占比 80%， 替代料占比20%。

订单数量200， 那系统会固定按 主料 200*80%= 160， 替代料 200 *20%= 40，这时进行可用性检查，只会检查库存数量是否满足，但不会改变订单中的组件数量。

entry.title: Re: Compensation Statement integration with FileNet using...
entry.content:
Hi @pavan0801 

As long as you integrate your data with APIs from any source system to SAP SuccessFactors you can then leverage one of the three ways of creating statements below:

  1. Standard compensation module compensation statement: requires data shown on statements to be on Compensation module worksheets (comp worksheets can get data from EC or MDF objects)
  2. Custom compensation module compensation : same data restrictions than above but can create custom layout and customer branding. 
  3. Document Generator (platform feature): data can be pulled directly from EC or MDF objects. Can be generated by employees themselves or mass generated by admins and sent by emails to employees.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
@fhreis 
Hello,
There is no automatic release process. The sales documents get approved credit status if the credit check passes, blocked if it fails and released if a user is releasing from credit block.
The static credit check (010) should set the credit status to approved on your first document which is under the credit limit and then block the second document. Maybe in your case you want to check on delivery level and only update the credit values starting from delivery level as well.
Without knowing the full business requirement we cannot answer this, but please note that the comment section here is not really suitable to help you with a query like this. You could open a separate thread on the SAP Community to share more on your setup and what is your requirement.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Learning Hub available to individuals?
entry.content:
Hi @Lorie_Schenck_SRP 
To access the guided, premium learning resources, you need to have either an SAP Learning Hub or SAP Learning Hub, partner edition (available only for SAP partners) subscription.
The SAP Learning Hub can also be purchased by individuals.
Please note that as an individual purchaser (B2I), customer can only make purchases by accepting SAP B2B Terms & Conditions.
As an individual purchaser, the customer should book the license under an organization (using their name as the company name and their home address as the postal address). For VAT purposes, the customer can use a dummy VAT registration number in their country's specific format.
Please follow the link provided here and fill out the information as mentioned above to proceed with placing the order.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: VAR case forwarding via SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API
entry.content:
Hello Florent,

Can JIRA make REST API requests? If yes, then you will need to configure it to make calls to the SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API. This information was mentioned in the original response:
"Your external ticketing software may be able to execute REST API requests. Refer to the external ticketing software documentation to check if it offers this functionality, and how to configure it."

The BTP destinations are required because the SAP Cloud ALM tenant will use them to perform the actual communication with the SAP Support Backbone. SAP Cloud ALM acts as a facilitator, of sorts. In the context of VARs, the only special considerations are the contents of the JSON included in the API requests. This is again also mentioned in the original response:
"A VAR Customer uses their End Customer's data in most of these properties. (E.G. "customer" is an S-user ID from the End Customer, "installationNumber" is the End Customer's installation number, etc.) The "reporter" property must be an S-user ID from the VAR itself. The resulting Case will be marked as a VAR Case in the Support Backbone."

Here is the flow of events when the SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API is called. They are the same for both normal Customers and for VARs. The only difference is the content of the JSON body.

  1. An external software submits a request to the SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API, using the URL pointing to a specific SAP Cloud ALM tenant. If the request is of method "POST", it must include JSON body content also.
    • The external software can be
      • A REST API client (e.g. Postman, Insomnia, Bruno, cURL).
      • A third party ticketing system with the ability to make REST API requests.
      • Middleware or other external software that can make REST API requests.
    • The JSON body content will be different depending on the Customer type.
      • A "normal" (non-VAR) Customer uses only their own data in these properties. (E.G. both "customer" and "reporter" are an S-user ID from their own company authorized to create a Case, etc.)
      • A VAR Customer uses their End Customer's data in most of these properties. (E.G. "customer" is an S-user ID from the End Customer, "installationNumber" is the End Customer's installation number, etc.) The "reporter" property must be an S-user ID from the VAR itself. The resulting Case will be marked as a VAR Case in the Support Backbone.
  2. The SAP Cloud ALM tenant receives the request.
  3. The SAP Cloud ALM tenant checks for the presence of the prerequisite BTP destinations ("calm_itsm_support", "calm_itsm_documents_services") in the "SAP Cloud ALM" Subaccount.
  4. If the destinations exist, the SAP Cloud ALM tenant internally routes the data from the request to the SAP Support Backbone via those destinations. When the Backbone request has completed, a Response is returned with an HTTP status code to indicate the success, as well as a relevant JSON payload.
  5. If the destinations do not exist then the API request will fail, return an appropriate HTTP status code, and possibly a JSON payload.

I hope that this clarifies the use of the API further.


Thanks and regards,
John

entry.title: Re: System is not allowing to update Valid to date for a...
entry.content:
The field 'valid to' date in the BOM is calculated by the system default with the date of 31.12.9999.
Without a change number the 'valid to' date will always be set to 31.12.9999.

If you change a BOM header or an item with a change number then the old database record gets a new 'valid to' date and accordingly a new 'valid from' date for the new entry.

entry.title: Dynamic Derivation of System ID from Application Server Directory (AL11)
entry.content:
Background

There are certain cases where other non-SAP applications will upload the file as Inbound in Application Server which needs to be loaded into BW. We have dedicated path created in AL11 where these Inbound files are placed and to be more precise, we define the directory path name based on System Landscape (DEV, QAT, PRD). This is done for the purpose to know exactly in which system either Inbound files are placed, or Outbound files are generated via OHD or any Custom Abap Programs.

For Example, the AL11 path name in DEV Environment is /usr/sap/actual/temp/ABD/test.txt

The AL11 path name in Quality Environment is /usr/sap/actual/temp/ABQ/test.txt

The AL11 path name in Production Environment is /usr/sap/actual/temp/ABP/test.txt

 

Here the System ID for DEV Environment is ABD

Here the System ID for Quality Environment is ABQ

Here the System ID for Production Environment is ABP

 

Scenario -1 (Info-package Routines for loading Inbound Flat Files)

Loading Data into BW via Dynamic Path in Info-package level where System ID is derived dynamically based on the System Landscape.

Below is the Syntax to dynamically derive the System ID.

CONCATENATE '/usr/sap/actual/temp/' sy-sysid '/test.txt' INTO p_filename.

 

Scenario -2 (Open Hub Destinations)

Generating the Outbound files via Open Hub Destination in Application Server path. Below is the Syntax of the Physical path under the Logical File Path Definition to dynamically derive the System ID. T. code is FILE.

/usr/sap/actual/temp/<SYSID>/<FILENAME>

 

Scenario -3 (Operating System Command)

Sometimes we need to execute a Script which is placed in the Application Server via System Command. This System Command is created in T. Code – SM69. It's being used to create/maintain external operating system commands.

Below is the Syntax to dynamically derive the System ID in Operating System Command definition to locate the exact path.

/usr/sap/actual/temp/$/SAPSYSTEMNAME//scripts/test.sh

entry.title: Tax Determination from Purchase Order to Logistics Invoice Verification.
entry.content:
Tax Determination in Purchase Orders to Invoice Verification

Case 1- Purchase Order without any Taxes.

Step 1: Refer to SAP standard Condition Table 4AV, M/05.

Step 2: SAP has provided a default access sequence MWST to access the above table.

Step 3: Create new Tax condition type  ZWVS copying from MWVS. (Tcode OBQ1) and assign the access sequence MWST.

Step 4: Assign new condition type ZWVS  to the existing tax procedure TAXINN.

Tcode- OBYZ

Insert the newly created condition type ZWVS into the tax pricing procedure with account Key VST.

Step 5: Create Tax codes in FTXP and assign 0 in ZWVS.

Once tax code is created in FTXP system will automatically make an entry in the tax condition record, FV11 in the Tax Classification.

Step 6: Assign Company Code to Tax codes for Non Taxable Transactions. 

Step 7: Maintain the Tax code V0 and the corresponding input tax GL created in OB40.

Step 8: PO creation with V0 tax code.

Step 9: MIRO posted with update in Purchase Order history-  

Accounting document in MIRO. 

Case 2-

Deductible Tax: 

Incase User manually enters the Tax code in Purchase Order with 18% Tax value.

Step 1: Create a new tax code V1 for 18% Tax and assign 18% on Input Taxes Tax Type (ZWVS). The tax amount will be booked on the Tax GL account and credit would be taken.

Step 2: Tax code to be maintained in OB40.

Step 3: Create PO with V1 with 18% Tax code.

In PO Pricing NAVS does not get updated in this case.

Step 4: Perform MIGO and the corresponding MIGO Entries are –

Accounting Entries in MIGO –

  • Inventory Account BSX + DR.
  • GR IR Clearing WRX -CR.

Step 5:  Perform MIRO –

Accounting Entries during MIRO –

  • GR IR Clearing WRX +DR
  • Input Tax VST +DR
  • Vendor Account -CR.

Case 3- Nondeductible Tax value.

Incase User manually enters the Tax code in Purchase Order with 18%.

Tax amount will add up to the inventory cost  during GRN and no credit on Tax would be taken.

Step 1: Create a new condition type ZMWV copying from condition type MWVY and assign access sequence MWST.

Step 2: Assign the condition type to the TAXINN pricing procedure and the accrual key should be NVV.

Step 3: Create Tax Code ND using Tcode FTXP

Tax classification Condition record automatically created in FV11.

Note** No entry in OB40 is required.

Step 4: PO Creation with ND Tax Code.

PO Pricing NAVS automatically gets updated with the Tax amount.

Moreover, you have to take into account that the non-deductible tax amount is calculated with function module 'CALCULATE_TAX_ITEM' program-internally.

Step 5: During GRN the tax amount will get inventorized to the Inventory GL. 

Inventory BSX DR+

GR/IR Clearing WRX CR –

Step 6: Perform MIRO-

Accounting Entries during MIRO –

  • GR/IR Clearing DR +
  • Vendor A/C CR -

Regards,

Sagnik.

entry.title: Error Recipient role with id '/RULE/MMPUR_MGR_L_APPR' cannot be resolved and how to resolve it
entry.content:
SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud contains several Manage Workflows Apps which can be used to set up Approval Workflows for different scenarios, for example for Purchase Requisitions. Within these Apps it is possible to set up Workflows Steps which should be performed by the 'Manager of Last Approver'.

With this definition 'Manager of Last Approver', we were facing some issues because not each CB User contains a manager.

Of course, if possible the resolution would be to maintain the manager in the Work Agreement.

However, for users without managers (like a CEO), there is no manager to be maintained. These Workflow items might end up in an error state and contain the error code 'Recipient role with id '/RULE/MMPUR_MGR_L_APPR' cannot be resolved':

 

If this error occurs for you, here is the solution:

Approval Steps for 'Manager of Last Approver' can be set as "optional":

"You can mark a workflow step as optional using the step property, <Is Optional Step>. This is an optional field, and by default a step is considered as mandatory. If a workflow step is marked optional and an approver cannot be determined for the step then the step gets skipped, and the workflow automatically moves to the next step, if any."

After creating a new workflow, the affected workflow items need to be re-triggered.

 

See also: Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions | SAP Help Portal; Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Business Partner Synchronization: defining the phases of conversion
entry.content:
Introduction

When migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA, there are preparatory steps that need to be done before the actual system conversion. There may be some clients that even prefer to have the pre-conversion steps implemented through pre-projects before closing the deal with the ultimate conversion project, as mentioned here. This is widely recommended especially because some of these steps don’t affect the day-to-day system functionality and can be implemented without impact on SAP ERP. Nonetheless, in all possible project scenarios, the customer/vendor synchronization is mandatory as a pre-conversion step for S/4HANA conversion due to the Business Partner Approach, meaning that the business partner is now the leading object for legal entities. Being so, the BP synchronization can be achieved by following the steps of the well-known KBA 2713963. When doing this activity for a big pharmaceutical client in Brazil, I have faced a complex scenario where I had to elaborate a flowchart to facilitate the definition of phases of conversion, which appeared difficult to assess at first glance. In this blog post I am going to disclose details about this flowchart to help others that may have the same complex scenario.

 

The scenarios

1. Keep the same numbers between BP and customer/vendor 

A question that must be answered is if there is a desire to keep the business partner and the customer/vendor with the same number. This means that when maintaining the entity’s master data in transaction BP, the number entered is the same used in the creation of transactional documents.

In the above example, customer 11 has the same number as its BP, but the vendor doesn’t. If it is not mandatory to keep the same one, then the BP range number can be configured independently, and the BP conversion is not complex in terms of number assignment. But if there is the desire to keep all possible numbers the same, then an analysis must be done of the current account groups range number customizing, using transaction OBAR and OBAS together with XDN1 and XKN1, which may culminate in the next scenarios.

2. Customers/vendors out of the current customized number range

As well described in SAP documentations about BP conversion, all created customers/vendors are scope of the conversion, even though they are marked for deletion, unless they are archived. This means that in some scenarios, the number of an old customer or vendor may be out of date, because its account group number range could have been changed through time.

The result is existing customers/vendors with a number range different than the current customizing for its account group in OBAR/OBAS, like in the above example, where the customer highlighted is a DEBI, which is configured to numerate from 900000 to 999999, therefore its number “1” is out of date. If this is part of the project scenario, then these entities with old range numbers can have their number reassigned*.

*Of course, the number reassignment is only an option. Even though the number may be out of the current customized range, one can always do a temporary configuration just to run the BP synchronization for these customers/vendors and then configure back to the current customizing. But this could result in several phases of conversion in production system, that may not be feasible in certain clients. Also, as standard the transaction BP shows a warning message when the number range of the opened BP is out of the current number range customized, so this may not be desirable.

3. Customers and vendors with same number

The last check that can be made is if there are customers and vendors that do not belong to the same legal entity but have the same number. This happens because the account groups for customers are different elements in SAP than the account groups for vendors, meaning that they can have the same number range.

As the business partner is now the leading object and have only one central number range configured, if a customer and vendor have overlapping numbers and do not belong to the same legal entity, one of them would need to have its number reassigned, because only one corresponding BP with this number can be created. 

 

Phases of conversion

The result is two amounts of customers/vendors to be converted: those that are going to have its number kept, and those that are going to have its number reassigned. The BP conversion for these entities must be done in two phases, because they demand different number range configuration. Note that the steps for the CVI configuration regarding number ranges are very well documented on SAP S/4HANA Cookbook CVI, so in the next topics only the specific customizing are going to be commented. 

1. Customers/vendors that are going to keep the same numbers in BP

To run the synchronization in this phase, the flag “External” must be checked in BP number range transaction BUCF, and the “Same number” field must be flagged in the assignment between BP grouping and account groups, in transactions V_TBC001 and V_TBD001.There is a KBA 24303080 describing this scenario. After running the BP conversion, then the next phase can be started.

2. Customers/vendors that are going to have the BP number reassigned

In this phase, the remaining entities are going to have their number defined internally according to the BP number range customizing, so the flags described in the previous phase must be unflagged.After finishing this conversion, it is likely that for the account groups that share the same range number with BP groupings, the numbers used in BP are different than the used in account group, meaning that for the same range, the “NR Status” is different between BUCF and XDN1/XKN1.It is highly important that these numbers be equalized after the conversion. 

An important observation is to make sure to use CVI_COCKPIT filters to capture only the desired customers/vendors for each phase, being the selection by number the most recommended. Additionally, as after the conversion any new customer or vendor created in XK01 and XD01 will synchronize a BP automatically, a final request must be transported to production system to make sure those flags are back in place for the automatic BP creation. Finally, note that the BP number is only relevant to the transaction BP, meaning that the number used in transactional documents is untouched. 

 

Conclusion

In this blog post it was possible to establish a flow of questions and decisions to achieve the phases of conversion in a BP synchronization step as a SAP ECC to S/4HANA pre-conversion project.

This flowchart can be used as a guide to analyze the scope of customers/vendors' number range in client’s scenario.  

As mentioned before, the CVI conversion is very well documented by SAP and the steps of configuration and synchronization are described accurately in the official Cookbook. It is recommended that one reach this CVI Cockpit FAQ, as this is a very complete blog post that contains answers to most of possible scenarios that a client could have. 

I hope I helped you organize the thoughts regarding the range numbers on complex master data scenarios based on an experience that I had, please comment below if you have any further. questions.

entry.title: Enhancing Name Formatting in SuccessFactors with Business Rules
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Knowledge Article 2685847 highlights a common issue: the system’s inability to format multi-word names effectively. However, real-world scenarios often require first name as well as last name with proper capitalization (e.g., "Jean Paul"). This document presents a scalable solution leveraging business rules to overcome this limitation, ensuring names are formatted correctly with capitalized first letters for each word.

Problem Statement

  • Limitation: The existing solution only supports single-word capitalize first character of the name.
  • Impact: Multi-word names leading to data inconsistency and poor user experience.
  • Requirement: A mechanism to process multi-word names and capitalize the first letter of each word (e.g., "jean paul" → "Jean Paul").

Proposed Solution: Business Rules for Name Formatting

Using business rule, we can create a rule to process and format name fields dynamically.

Key Features

  • Handles single-word names (e.g., "jean" → "Jean").
  • Formats multi-word names (e.g., "jean paul" → "Jean Paul").
  • Ensures consistency across all name fields in the system.

Implementation Steps

Step 1: Define the Business Rule

Assign this rule to the first name field in HRIS element personalInfo.

Step 2: Testing and Validation

  • Test cases:
    • Single word: "jean" → "Jean"
    • Two words: "jean paul" → "Jean Paul"
    • Edge cases: "JEAN PAUL " → "Jean Paul", "jEaN pAUl" → "Jean Paul"

Input: Raw name string (e.g., "jean paul").

Output: Formatted name string (e.g., "Jean Paul").

Benefits

  • Flexibility: Supports two-word names.
  • Consistency: Enforces uniform capitalization across all name fields.
  • Scalability: Easily extensible to other text fields requiring similar formatting.

Conclusion

By leveraging business rules, users can bypass the single-word name formatting limitation outlined in Knowledge Article 2685847. This whitepaper provides a practical, reusable solution to format names with proper capitalization, enhancing data quality and user satisfaction. Future enhancements to use function replace() could include support for hyphenated names (e.g., "Jean-Pierre") or cultural naming conventions.

References

  • SAP Knowledge Article 2685847: Link

entry.title: Efficient Workflow Management: How to Streamline Stalled Workflow Reminder at Scale
entry.content:
Have you ever found yourself dealing with stalled workflows that hold up critical tasks? While it's easy to adjust a few workflows, what happens when you have hundreds of them? The thought of manually tracking and sending reminders for all those workflows can quickly become overwhelming. But it doesn't have to be that way.

In this article, we'll show you how to streamline the process and create an efficient solution that saves you time and effort.

Without wasting any time, let's dive straight into it. Before we implement the solution, there are few important things you should know. 

First, there are specific permissions required to proceed with the stepup. You'll need to have access to the Scheduled Job Manager. If you don't have this permission you can grant it, below is the process.

  1. Login to SAP SuccessFactors instance.
  2. Navigation to Admin Center -> Manage Permission Roles.
  3. Select the role for whom you want to grant access, Click on Edit.
  4. Under Administration Permission -> Admin Center Permissions. Check both the options Monitor Scheduled Jobs and Manage Scheduled Jobs.
  5. Click on Next and Save it.

Logout and logged in for testing.

To Send Workflow reminders efficiently, you can schedule a job using the Manage Scheduled Jobs feature. There are two ways to create a job:-

  • Provisioning
  • Admin Center

Since every consultant or admin doesn't have provisioning access, in this blog, we'll focus on how you can create a job directly from the Admin Center (Instance).

Here is the Step by Step Guide:- 

Step 1: Login to your SAP SuccessFactors Instance.

Step 2: Navigate to Admin Center -> Scheduled Job Manager.

Step 3: Click on Job Scheduler and after that on the right side, Click on + Create Job Request.

Step 4: Under Job Definition, Fill the required information as mentioned. 

  • Job Name: Fill as per your requirement. In this case, giving name as Workflow Reminder Notifications.
  • Job Type: Select as Workflow Action Reminder.
  • Job Owner: Who will receive all email related to job. Ideally it should be from Admin team or Super admin,
  • Job Parameters: Remind in Days, If you define any number here, System will send the reminder to approver in that interval time or if you wish the system should follow the configuration defined in Workflow, Leave it as blank.

In our requirement, we need to set it as 1.

  • Include Workflow Initiator in Workflow Reminders: As per your requirements you can select the checkbox.

In General, It's not required because it sends the reminder every time to initiator irrespective of their action on the workflow.

 

Step 5: UnderJob Occurence, Fill the information as per your requirement.

  • Occurence: Depends on your requirement, you can select.
    • One Time: The job will run only once.
    • Recurring: Job will run repeatedly, often at regular intervals defined by you.

Here our requirement is to send a daily reminder, we will select Recurring. 

  • Recurrence Pattern: Select any of the option available in the dropdown list.
    • Daily: It will run on daily basis.
    • Weekly:Job will run once in a week.
    • Monthly:Job will run once in a month.
    • Yearly: Job will run once in a year.

In our requirement, we need to run the job on daily basis. We are selecting Daily here.

  • Start: From when the Job will start.
  • End: Define the end date for job.

Step 6: Under Notification, Fill the required details.

  • Recipients: If you want to send a job update to any person except job owner, you can add thier mail id here.
  • Send Emails When Job Starts: If you want to send an email every time when the job starts, you can check this box. Ideally select this option only when the occurence is once. If you check this box and Occurence is recurrring, daily pattern, you will end with so many emails in your inbox.
  • Send Emails When Job Completes: Once the job is complete, System will send the notification over email.
  • Send Emails When Job Fails: This option is greyed out, as it is important if any job fails admin must be aware of that. 

Step 7: On the bottom right corner, Click on Save.

Step 8: Recheck all your configuration before submitting your job. You wouldn't be able to edit your job after submitting it.

Step 9: On Job Page, Against your job Click on Actions and Submit.

Step 10: If you want to run the job immediately. After Submitting the Job, click on Action and select Run It Now. 

Result:

After submitting the job, you can check the status under job monitor. If it's not visible under Job Monitor, you can check on Upcoming Jobs section.

entry.title: Managing Vacation Carryovers with customer-specific requirements in SAP SuccessFactors: A Step-by-St
entry.content:
In many organizations, employees can carry over unused vacation days into the following year. However, effectively managing carryover rules in SAP SuccessFactors can be challenging—especially when each company’s requirements vary significantly. Building on the foundation of various existing resources (such as this excellent overview), I’d like to present a specific solution that lets you transfer leftover vacation into a dedicated carryover account based on your own defined conditions.

Key Benefits

  • Separate Carryover Account: Unused vacation appears in its own account, distinct from the following year’s regular entitlement.
  • Clear Yearly Breakdown: Each carryover balance is shown separately (a legal requirement in some countries, such as Germany).
  • First-In-First-Out Usage: You can configure the system so that the oldest accrued days are used first.
  • Flexible Eligibility: Permissions and rules around who may carry over time can be tailored to your organization’s needs.

 

How to Implement

  1. Add a Custom Field to the Time Account Object

In Configure Object Definitions, add a decimal-type custom field (for example, “Carry Over Balance”) to store the leftover vacation balance at the end of the base account’s validity period.

 

  1. Create a Period-End Business Rule in the Base Time Account Type

For your base (primary) time account type (e.g., the “Vacation” account), create a Business Rule that triggers at the Period End. This rule calculates any unused vacation balance, stores it in the custom “Carry Over Balance” field, and then closes the account so it cannot be used beyond its end date.

Assign this rule under Period-End Processing Rule in the base time account type.

  1. Set Up a Dedicated Carryover Time Account

For this use case, we do not want the carryover to be transferred to the vacation account of the following year, but we want to save it in a separate carryover account, so that it can be displayed separately. Therefore, create a new Time Account Type (e.g., “Carryover Account”) and assign it to the same Time Type used by your base account.

Make sure to adjust the Posting Priority in the Time Type to “Post to time accounts by posting rules only” and ensure the carryover account is listed before the base account in under Time Account Posting Rules.

 

  1. Define an Accrual Rule for the Carryover Account

To ensure the carryover account is correctly populated, configure a new Accrual Business Rule that references the “Carry Over Balance” stored in the base account’s custom field. Within this rule, you can define precisely when a carryover should occur, allowing you to establish multiple conditions. In this example, the entitlement is carried over if the employee was absent at the end of the base time account—meaning they were unable to use or schedule their vacation—or if any other special circumstances apply (as determined by HR).

 

How the rule works:

  • The first IF condition checks whether an employee was absent at the end of the original time account. Specifically, it calculates the absence days within a defined period (in this case, March 30 to March 31 of the current year) for selected absence types (which you can configure as needed).
  • The second IF condition checks a custom HRIS field in the employee profile, which was created in Job Info (via Manage Business Configuration) and linked to a simple Yes/No picklist called “Carryover Eligible.” This lets HR determine if an employee could not take their vacation for other reasons (e.g., operational) and is therefore entitled to a carryover.

Note: This post does not cover the detailed creation of custom HRIS fields; there are other excellent resources for that.

  • In the THEN definition, you specify what happens to the carryover if the employee is indeed eligible. For the Posting Date, you can use the variable time.account valid from, which denotes the start date of the carryover account. The Amount Posted configuration is a bit more involved: to reference the correct original account, use a Lookup function (object = Time Account). Within that Time Account, select the “Carry Over Balance” field created in step one. Then define which specific account’s balance should be referenced by ensuring the user matches the one in the current carryover account, the Time Account Type equals “Vacation,” and the account’s start and end dates fall between January 1 and December 31 of the previous year. This ensures the system retrieves only the most recent base account rather than one from an earlier period.

 

Final Thoughts

With these configurations in place, leftover vacation days are automatically calculated, transferred into a dedicated carryover account, and made clearly distinguishable for both employees and administrators. This not only reduces the manual workload for HR but also aligns with legal and policy requirements around vacation entitlement transparency.

 

Have questions or need more details? Feel free to leave a comment!

Greetings from Germany
Sofia

 

Disclaimers

This is a custom solution. Please conduct thorough testing and exercise due diligence before deploying it in a production environment.

All data here is fictitious, any correspondence with real data is pure coincidence.

entry.title: No Business Does Business Alone
entry.content:
The market and our supplier community have been asking for more ways to grow their business on SAP Business Network and we are delivering. In October, we announced a new, exciting offering for suppliers on SAP Business Network. SAP Business Network, promote subscription has been designed to help suppliers better understand market opportunities, boost their discoverability, and generate sales with new and existing customers. We are so pleased to announce that on February 26th, promote subscription will now be available in a phased rollout on SAP Business Network. By the end of March, all suppliers will be granted access to the new network storefront to add the promote subscription.

In the time since the announcement, we’ve heard very positive feedback from suppliers about the new offering. Our SAP buyer customers are also excited to tap into SAP Business Network to find new sources of supply. Supplier search has been enhanced, and buyers can now search supplier catalogs in addition to supplier profiles.  

One of the biggest asks has been about the enhanced supplier experience with the promote subscription. Here's a quick look at how SAP Business Network, promote subscription enhances the features of your supplier account on the network to help your company stand out and get discovered:

All suppliers can join SAP Business Network and build a profile at no cost. For those suppliers looking to take their discoverability to the next level, promote subscription is now available. So, whether you’ve been on the network for years or you’re just getting started, all suppliers looking for new business growth will benefit when they subscribe. Afterall; no business does business alone.

Learn more at:  www.sap.com/promotesubscription

entry.title: Query by Product internal process using Radio Frequency User Interface
entry.content:
Product:
In SAP, a product refers to any material or goods that are tracked and managed in the system.
Products are defined by their unique attributes, such as material number, description, and
specifications. SAP allows for efficient management of products throughout the supply chain,
including procurement, inventory, and sales.


RFUI (Radio Frequency User Interface):
RFUI in SAP is the mobile interface used to guide warehouse workers through tasks like
picking, packing, and inventory management using handheld devices. It enables real-time
communication with the system, improving efficiency and accuracy in warehouse operations.
RFUI supports streamlined workflows and data capture directly at the point of activity.


Internal Process:
Internal processes in SAP encompass the series of steps within the organization that handle
goods movement, inventory management, and data flow, such as receiving, storing, and
transferring products. These processes ensure the seamless movement of materials within the
warehouse and between systems. SAP optimizes internal processes through automation,
improving operational efficiency and inventory accuracy.

 

Process for Query by Product internal process using RFUI

 

Step1:

Navigating to the RFUI screen using the transaction code.

Then navigate to the process flow below

Internal processes-> 04 Stock/ Bin Query -> 01 Stock / Bin Query by Product

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step2:

Provide the Product details:

 

 

 

 

Then click on the product details button.

The Products details such as Storage bin, Quantity and Unit of measure are displayed.

 

Conclusion:
Process for Query by Product internal process using Radio Frequency User Interface is provided

entry.title: Central PLS Creation and Management (Transportation Cockpit Central Management)
entry.content:
Central PLS Creation and Management

The PLS management screens enable an administrator to centrally create and manage PLSs for a team or organization and assign users to them. This approach simplifies the process of working with PLSs by removing the need for individual users to create and maintain their own.

When an administrator creates a PLS and assigns a user to it, the PLS is available for the user to select on the relevant application start screen.

The administrator can create and manage PLSs by choosing from the following in SAP Business Client under Application Administration  Planning  General Settings  Profile and Layout Sets: Profile and Layout Set Management for Transportation Cockpit

Fiori Application

One can assign / remove created Profile and Layout sets to team members.

 

Regards,

Chinmay Joshi.

entry.title: Batch, Serial, and Quality Management in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Managing electronic components demands precision, accuracy, and stringent quality control. Without an efficient system, tracking these products can become overwhelming, leading to risks such as counterfeits, defective parts, and inventory discrepancies. SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) simplifies this process through Serial Number Management, Batch Management, and Quality Control, ensuring streamlined and efficient warehouse operations.

Serial Number Management in SAP EWM ensures precise tracking by assigning unique serial numbers to items, enabling real-time traceability, warranty tracking, and counterfeit prevention at the inventory level. Batch Management organizes stock based on production batches, utilizing FIFO and FEFO strategies to optimize inventory handling, prevent obsolescence, and improve order accuracy. Quality Management safeguards product integrity by triggering inspections upon goods receipt, blocking defective stock, and enabling returns or rework to ensure only high-quality products enter production or reach customers.

Let’s dive into the configuration to achieve this requirement!

Configuration:

1. Specify Batch Level and Activate Status Management

2. Maintain Internal Batch Number Assignment Range

3. Define LE Serial Number Profile

4. Define Serial Number Profile (Other SAP Components)

5. Define Warehouse Number Dependent Serial Number Profiles

6.Serial Number Settings for Warehouse Number

7. Quality Management

  I. Generate Inspection object types versions

 II. Maintain Inspection object type

III. Maintain Follow Up Actions

IV. Assign Logistical Follow up Actions

 V. S4- Maintain Inspection Types

VI. S4 – Maintain Settings at Plant Level

VII. S4- Define Follow-up Actions for Usage Decision

VIII. S4-Edit Code Groups and code for usage Decisions

 IX. S4- Edit Selected sets for usage decisions

8. Warehouse Process Type

1. Define Warehouse Process Type

2. Define process type determination indicator

3. Determine Warehouse Process Type

9.Putaway Strategies

1. Define STSS for Put away

2. Assign Storage Types to STSS

3. Specify Storage type search sequence for put away

10. Create Material (Serial, Batch & Quality Managed)

The screenshots provided pertain exclusively to the serial, batch, and quality management parameters that must be maintained in the material.

Extend this material to Product master data in SAP EWM and extend storage location as well.

Test Case

1. Create Purchase Order- ME21N

2. Create Outbound delivery & distribute to EWM- VL01N

With reference to the PO, an Inbound Delivery is created, including Batch and Serial Numbers. As a prerequisite, the batch must first be created using the transaction code MSC1N, as shown in the screenshot below.

 

If the vendor sends an Advance Shipping Notice (ASN) containing batch and serial number information, the system automatically creates a batch and assigns the corresponding serial numbers to the respective inbound delivery. In this scenario, manually created a batch and entered the details before assigning the serial number to the respective delivery. This ensures proper tracking and alignment of batch and serial information within the system.

3. Maintain Outbound delivery in EWM- /N/SCWM/PRDO

The delivery is distributed to EWM along with the batch and serial numbers. In SAP EWM, batches and serial numbers can also be created independently. If a batch is created in EWM, it is automatically replicated in S/4HANA to maintain consistency. Similarly, serial numbers generated in EWM are also distributed to S/4HANA, ensuring synchronization between both systems for seamless inventory tracking and management.

As soon as the goods receipt is posted in SAP EWM, the system automatically generates an inspection document, as shown in the image below. This inspection document is created because the product is quality-managed, ensuring that it undergoes the necessary quality checks before being released into inventory.

4. Inspection Lot- QA32

Since this product is batch-managed, you can search for the inspection lot using the batch number when making a usage decision. Once usage decision saved in inspection lot, An warehouse task will be created in EWM for put away.

5. Warehouse Monitor- /N/SCWM/MON

The warehouse tasks are generated based on the follow-up action configured in Step 7 (IV). The assigned Warehouse Process Type within the follow-up action triggers the creation of these tasks. Once these tasks are confirmed, the products are efficiently moved to their designated storage bins, ensuring seamless warehouse operations and accurate inventory placement. If the products are rejected, they will be moved to a scrap storage type. However, if they are returnable, they will be transferred to a quarantine storage area for temporary holding before being returned at a later stage.

In conclusion, managing electronic components efficiently requires a system that ensures tracking, quality control, and accurate inventory management. SAP EWM helps achieve this through Serial Number Management, Batch Management, and Quality Control, making warehouse operations smoother and more reliable. By using these features, businesses can reduce risks, prevent counterfeits, minimize waste, and improve efficiency. This ensures that only high-quality products make it to customers, strengthening the overall supply chain.

entry.title: Enabling Generative AI in SAP BTP: Integrating AI Core, AI Launchpad, and API Readiness
entry.content:
Introduction

Generative AI is revolutionizing enterprise applications by enabling automation, predictive insights, and intelligent decision-making. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides a powerful ecosystem to integrate AI capabilities seamlessly, but unlocking its full potential requires configuring key services like SAP AI Core and AI Launchpad.

In this blog post, we’ll walk you through the process of enabling generative AI in SAP BTP by integrating AI Core for model execution, leveraging AI Launchpad for lifecycle management, and preparing AI-driven APIs for consumption by microservices. By the end of this guide, you’ll have a clear roadmap to operationalize AI in your SAP landscape—ready to enhance business applications.

Let’s get started! 🚀

Enabling Entitlements

The first step is to enable the necessary entitlements for your SAP BTP subaccount. To do this, navigate to your subaccount and select "Entitlements" from the left-hand navigation menu. Then, click "Edit" at the top, followed by "Add Service Plan".

In the search field, type "AI" and select the following services:

  • SAP AI Core with the "standard" and "sap-internal" plans
  • SAP AI Launchpad with the "standard" plan

(Disclaimer: If you cannot find both services in the "Add Service Plans" list, you may need to use the "SAP BTP Control Center" to assign the appropriate quotas to your Global Account first. If necessary, please contact your Global Account Administrator for assistance.).

Once the services are selected, click "Add Service Plans". The updated Entitlements list should now include SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad.

Adding AI-Core Service to a space

Navigate to a space within the subaccount where the entitlements have been set, and select "Instances" from the left menu. On the right side of the screen, click "Create", then choose "Service Instance".

In the dialog, select SAP AI Core as the service, choose either sap-internal or standard as the plan, and assign it a meaningful name. In our case, we select the sap-internal plan as it meets all our requirements.

Once the service is created, select it from the list and click the "Create" button under "Service Keys".

On the next screen, give the key a name and click 'Create.' Once the key is created, you can open it to view the credentials for the AI Core API. We'll need these later, so make a note of the following fields from the key:

  • clientid
  • clientsecret
  • url
  • AI_API_URL

We will use the 'clientid' and 'clientsecret' to authenticate with the AI API. The 'url' is the authentication endpoint, and 'AI_API_URL' is the main domain followed by specific sub-URIs that will be used to send requests to our LLM later.

Adding SAP AI Launchpad to the Subaccount

Next, we need to activate the AI Launchpad to configure our LLMs and deployment. While this can technically be done using the SAP AI Service we enabled in the previous step, we prefer using a UI for a clearer understanding of the process.

Navigate to your Subaccount and select 'Instances & Subscriptions' from the left menu. Click the 'Create' button on the right, select 'SAP AI Launchpad' as the service, and 'Standard' as the plan.

This will enable the Launchpad and also add new user roles to your subaccount. Before we can access the Launchpad, we need to assign some of the new roles to our user. Navigate to the 'Users' section in the left menu, which can be found under the 'Security' category. Search for the user you want to grant access to the Launchpad and assign the following roles:

  • ailaunchpad_allow_all_resourcegroups
  • ailaunchpad_connections_editor
  • ailaunchpad_functions_explorer_editor_v2
  • ailaunchpad_genai_administrator
  • ailaunchpad_mloperations_editor

These roles should provide your user with the necessary permissions to access the Launchpad and all its functionality to configure and deploy an LLM. The role names may change in the future, so slight adjustments might be needed.

Navigate back to 'Instances and Subscriptions,' select 'SAP AI Launchpad' from the list, and click the 'Go to Application' button on the right. Note: If the application opens with a 'Forbidden' response, try opening it in incognito mode, as this typically means the roles we just assigned require a new login.

Once in the Launchpad, the screen should look like the screenshot below.

Configuring and Deploying a Model

In the next steps, we will select an LLM and create a deployment for it. As mentioned in the previous chapter, all configuration is done through the SAP AI Core service, which we enabled in our space. This means we now need to add the service to the Launchpad so that we can manage it through a graphical user interface.

Click the 'Add' button on the right. In the 'Create API Connection' dialog, we will need to provide the credentials that we previously noted when creating the key for the SAP AI Core service.

  • Choose a meaningful connection name.
  • Leave the 'Service Key' field empty.
  • For the 'AI API URL,' enter the 'AI_API_URL' from the SAP AI Core service credentials.
  • For the 'XSUAA URL,' use the 'url' from the SAP AI Core service credentials.
  • For 'Client ID' and 'Client Secret,' use the 'clientid' and 'clientsecret' properties from the SAP AI Core service credentials.

Click the 'Create' button. If done correctly, the screen should now display the AI API Connection along with a 'default' resource group that has been created.

Next, we need to choose an LLM and deploy it. In the left-side menu, navigate to 'Generative AI Hub' and then to 'Model Library.' Choose a model and click 'Deploy' to start the deployment. In our case, we will be using GPT-4.

 

In the left menu, you will find the 'ML Operations' section. Expand it to discover many useful features.

  • Overview provides a small dashboard where you can view your deployments, scenarios, and more.
  • Scenarios should already include two pre-created scenarios: 'Orchestration' and 'Foundations-Models.' If they are not present, create a new one of type 'foundation-model.'
  • In Configurations, you should see a default configuration called 'gpt-4_autogenerated.' This is the default configuration for the model we selected earlier.
  • In Deployments, you should now see the model deployment we triggered earlier. As you can see, it is based on the 'gpt-4_autogenerated' configuration that we previously saw in the 'Configurations' section.

Select the deployment to view its details.

The deployment should have the status 'Running.' If it still shows the status 'Pending,' it means the deployment hasn't finished yet, and we'll need to wait a little longer. Once it is complete, take note of the URL at the top of the Deployment Details page. It will have the format: <AI_API_URL>/v2/inference/deployments/<DEPLOYMENT GUID>.

This URL can now be used to send requests to our LLM. Let's test it in Bruno, Postman, or any other API REST client.

Sending Requests with Bruno

Next, we need to create two requests in Bruno. The first request will retrieve a valid JWT that we can use for authorization in the second request, which will be the actual LLM request.

In Bruno, create a POST request to the URL you noted earlier from the AI-Core credentials. Use the same URL that you provided when connecting AI Core to the Launchpad, and append /oauth/token to the end of the URL.

Add a Form URL Encoded body to the request with the following properties:

  • grant_type: client_credentials
  • client_id: the clientid provided by AI Core credentials
  • client_secret: the clientsecret provided by AI Core credentials

Send the request, and you will receive a response containing an access_token.

Now, create a second POST request. This time, use the URL we noted in the deployment details view. Append chat/completions?api-version=2024-10-21 to the end of the URL.

In the headers, add the following:

  • Accept: application/json
  • ai-resource-group: default
  • Authorization: Bearer <insert the access_token from the previous request here>

Next, add the body in the following JSON format:

 

 

 

{
  "messages": [
        {
          "role": "user",
          "content": "Say Hello to SAP Community"
        }
  ],
  "max_tokens": 1000,
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "frequency_penalty": 0,
  "presence_penalty": 0,
  "stop": "null"
}

 

 

 

Now, when you send the request, you should receive a response from the AI greeting the SAP Community.

Since a REST API client can now communicate with our LLM through the AI Core service, it can easily be integrated into any microservices we may already have in BTP. We simply need to implement the same two requests as we just did.

NestJS coding example

This example will implement a 'hello' endpoint in a NestJS application and use Axios to send the requests.

 

 

 

import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AppService } from './app.service';

@Controller()
export class AppController {
  constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {}

  @Get('hello')
  async getHello(): Promise<string> {
    return await this.appService.getHello();
  }
}

// app.service.ts
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import axios from 'axios';

@Injectable()
export class AppService {
  private authUrl = '<url from AI Core Credentials>/oauth/token';
  private aiApiUrl = '<deployment url>/chat/completions?api-version=2024-10-21';
  private clientId = '<clientid from AI Core credentials>';
  private clientSecret = '<clientsecret from AI Core credentials>';

  async getHello(): Promise<string> {
    try {
      // First request: Get access token
      const authResponse = await axios.post(this.authUrl, {
        client_id: this.clientId,
        client_secret: this.clientSecret,
        grant_type: 'client_credentials',
      });
      const accessToken = authResponse.data.access_token;

      // Second request: Call AI API
      const aiResponse = await axios.post(
        this.aiApiUrl,
        {
          messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Say Hello to SAP Community' }],
          max_tokens: 1000,
          temperature: 0.0,
          frequency_penalty: 0,
          presence_penalty: 0,
          stop: 'null',
        },
        {
          headers: {
            Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`,
            'ai-resource-group': 'default',
          },
        },
      );
      
      return aiResponse.data.choices[0]?.message?.content || 'No response from AI';
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error calling AI API:', error);
      return 'Error retrieving response';
    }
  }
}

 

 

 

Summary

In this blog post, we walked through the essential steps to enable Generative AI capabilities in SAP BTP. We started by enabling entitlements on the BTP subaccount, ensuring that the required AI services were available. Next, we added the AI Core service to a space and integrated the SAP AI Launchpad into the subaccount to manage AI models efficiently.

We then moved on to configuring and deploying a model, covering key deployment steps to ensure it was ready for consumption. To test the setup, we used Bruno, a powerful API client, to send requests and validate AI responses. Finally, we implemented a NestJS coding example, demonstrating how to authenticate, retrieve an access token, and call the AI API to generate responses.

By following these steps, you can successfully integrate and utilize AI services within SAP BTP, enabling advanced AI-driven applications.

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Configure Currency Conversion in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Currency conversion in SAP IBP allows you to convert financial data from one currency to another, ensuring consistency and accuracy in your planning and reporting processes. Here's an overview of how it works:

  1. Exchange Rates: The system uses predefined exchange rates to convert values between different currencies. These rates can be time-dependent, meaning they can vary over different periods.

  2. Configuration: To set up currency conversion, you need to define currency attributes and create master data types for currencies and exchange rates. This involves specifying the original currency, the target currency, and the corresponding exchange rates.

  3. Key Figures: When defining key figures that require currency conversion, you specify the conversion logic. For example, you might convert sales forecast revenue from local currencies to a common reporting currency like USD.

  4. Real-Time Conversion: SAP IBP can perform currency conversion on the fly. This means that when users view data in the SAP IBP Excel add-in or analytics tools, they can select the target currency, and the system will automatically apply the appropriate exchange rates.

  5. Scenario Planning: You can use different exchange rate scenarios to analyze the impact of currency fluctuations on your financial plans. This helps in making informed decisions based on potential future changes in exchange rates.

By leveraging these features, SAP IBP ensures that your financial data is accurately represented across different currencies, facilitating better global planning and reporting.

Create Attributes

 

1. Create the attributes used for the currency conversion using the Attributes app. Use the values in the following table. Reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create an Attribute in SAP IBP

IDNameTypeLength
I49CURRIDCurrency FromNVARCHAR20
I49CURRDESCRCurrency DescriptionNVARCHAR40
I49CURRTOIDCurrency ToNVARCHAR20
I49CURRTODESCRCurrency To DescriptionNVARCHAR40
I49EXCHGRATEExchange RateDECIMAL18, 6

2. In the app Master Data Types, create the simple master data type I49CURRENCY using the following details. Reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Simple Master Data Type in SAP IBP

FieldValue
NameCurrency From
DescriptionCurrency From
Master Data TypeSimple
ATTRIBUTES: 
I49CURRIDSelected(Key)
I49CURRDESCRSelected

3. Create the reference master data type I49CURRENCYTO using the following data:

FieldValueReference Attribute
NameCurrency To 
DescriptionCurrency To 
Master Data TypeReference 
Reference Master Data TypeI49CURRENCY 
ATTRIBUTES:  
I49CURRTOIDKeyI49CURRID
I49CURRTODESCR I49CURRDESCR

4. Create the master data type I49EXCHANGERATE as a compound master data type of I49CURRENCY and I49CURRENCYTO using the following details. Reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Compound Master Data Type in SAP IBP

FieldValueSimple Master Data Types
NameExchange Rate 
DescriptionExchange Rate 
Master Data TypeCompound 
Add Simple Master Data Types: I49CURRENCY
  I49CURRENCYTO
ATTRIBUTES:  
I49CURRIDKey 
I49CURRTOIDKey 
I49EXCHGRATE  

Assign Currency Attributes to the Planning Area


Open the planning area and assign the simple master data types I49CURRENCY and I49CURRENCYTO to the planning area.

Create Planning Levels

1. Create the PERCURRCURRTO planning level, which will be the base planning level for the exchange rate. Reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a planning level in SAP IBP

Select lowest time profile level 1-Monthly.

Enter ID and Description.

Add PERIODID via Change Assignment in Time Attributes

Add Master Data Type, I49CURRENCY and I49CURRENCYTO in Attributes. Select Conversion Source and Conversion Target Indicator.

2. Create the PERPRODCUSTCURR planning level, which will be the base planning level for the Target Rev key figure.

Select lowest time profile level 1-Monthly.

Enter ID and Description 

Add PERIODID via Change Assignment in Time Attributes

Add Master Data Type, I49CURRENCY, I49CURRENCYTO, I49CUSTOMER and I49PRODUCT in Attributes.

3. Create the PERPRODCUSTCURR planning level, which will be the base planning level for the Target Rev key figure.

Select lowest time profile level 1-Monthly.

Enter ID and Description

Add PERIODID via Change Assignment in Time Attributes

Add Master Data Type, I49CURRENCY, I49CUSTOMER and I49PRODUCT in Attributes.

 

Assign the EXCHANGERATE Key Figure as an Attribute as Key Figure

1. Set attribute I49EXCHGRATE as key Figure and assign the PERCURRCURRTO planning level. Reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Key Figure Using an Attribute in SAP IBP

Go to Attributes as Key Figure tab and choose New. 

Search in the Attributes field and select I49EXCHANGERATE.

Assign the PERCURRCURRTO planning level.

Assign the From period, -6 and To period,  12.

Choose Save.

2. Make the following settings for the I49EXCHNGRATE key figure.

FieldValue
StoredX
Edit AllowedEditable in the Current or Future
Aggregation ModeAvg
Disaggregation ModeCopy Value

3. Check that the calculation of the Request level is the Avg of the PERCURRCURRTO level and the input is stored.

4. Create a new key figure called TARGETREVENUE using the following data.

FieldValue
Key Figure IDTARGETREVENUE
NameTarget Revenue
Base Planning LevelPERPRODCUSTCURR
StoredX
Edit AllowedEditable in the Current or Future
Aggregation ModeSum
Disaggregation ModeEqual distribution
ProportionalitySame Key Figure - Stored Values
Convert UsingI49EXCHGRATE

5. Add the calculation so the Target Revenue is calculated by the stored value times the exchange rate.

TARGETREVENUE@PERPRODCUSTCURRCURRTO = “TARGETREVENUE@PERPRODCUSTCURR” * “I49EXCHGRATE@PERCURRCURRTO”

6. Change the Request level calculation for TARGETREVENUE so that it is the sum of the PERPRODCUSTCURRCURRTO level.

TARGETREVENUE@REQUEST = SUM(“TARGETREVENUE@PERPRODCUSTCURRCURRTO”)

7. Activate planning area and select the Full Scope with Dependencies.

Load Data into the New Master Data Types and Key Figure

1. Load data into your new I49CURRENCY and the Exchange Rate master data types.

2. Load data into your Target Revenue key figure.

Please reference https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/technology-blog-members/article-id/171537?prePageCrumb=BlogUserContributedArticlesPage#:~:text=Load%20Master%20Data%20for%20Each%20Master%20Data%20Type%20in%20Planning%20Area

 

Test the Solution using Planner Workspace

1. Test your solution by creating a Workspace using the information from the table. After you view the data, choose Settings and change the currency to view the data in another currency.Use the following values for Time:

FieldValue
Time PeriodMonthly
FromCurrent Month
ToCurrent Month + 12
RollingYes

Use the following values for Attributes:

Field Value
Product IDSelected
Customer IDSelected
Currency To DescriptionSelected

Use the following values for Key Figures:

FieldValue
Target Revenue, Exchange RateSelected
I49CURRTOIDUSD

Change Currency To to EUR, the values are all changed as exchange rate

2. Test the from/to period setting for the exchange rate attribute as a key figure. Expand the workspace to include the last year, and check that the exchange rate is not included past the setting.

the exchange rate is not included beyond the last 6 months.

The last 6 months have only have values in exchange rate.

Because key figure I49EXCHGRATE sets From Period -6, To Period 12, when we upload data to I49EXCHGRATE, system will upload data from the last 6 months to the next 12 months.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Integration & Exception Monitoring alert editing role collection for L1/L2 support.
entry.content:
In SAP Cloud ALM Operations Integration & Exception Monitoring, our L1/L2 Support is assigned to a custom role collection which contains the role "ops_im_IntegrationOwner". But the users cannot perform any actions on Alerts. They should be able to at least perform these actions: 

  • Confirm
  • Add Comments
  • Assign Processors
  • Remove Processors.

We reviewed the documentation and saw that the next possible role would be "ops_im_IntegrationArchitect". But this also authorizes to adjust KPIs, thresholds etc. So it is unacceptable for our L1/L2 support.

How can we grant basic L1/L2 permissions for Monitoring Use Cases and alerts in SAP Cloud ALM, especially Integration & Exception Monitoring, without granting access to configuration tasks?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Integration & Exception Monitoring aler...
entry.content:
The "Integration Owner" role (ops_im_IntegrationOwner) is roughly equivalent to the "Viewer", "Consumer", and "Analyst" roles in other SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use cases (e.g. "Health Monitoring Viewer", "Job Monitoring Consumer ", and "Configuration Monitoring Analyst" respectively). The intended use case is that these users have read only access to the Operations use cases. This restriction also applies to the Alerting for those metrics. 

If you currently wish for users to have the ability to edit Alerts in the Operations use cases, they need to have the next tier of role collection for that use case. As you have already mentioned, for Integration & Exception Monitoring it is "Integration Architect". 

If you feel that this behaviour does not align with your business case or usage, and that a separate Role Collection should be created specially for L1/L2 type support (Read only, except for Alerts), please create a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Campaign in the SAP Customer Influence portal. If you outline your business case there, the Developers may provide a new role collection as per your requirement. If they decide to pursue it further, an item will be created in the Roadmap for SAP Cloud ALM with a projected delivery date. Then if it is delivered, the SAP Cloud ALM What's New page will be updated with the detailed information about how to use the new functionality.
