entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @MichaelFromNPB ,

this question is pretty much off-topic, as this blog was only about installing/spinning-up the mini-SAP, not about how to connect to the instance using JCo or otherwise. 

Anyway... since you can connect to the SAP instance using a Windows VM - we'll assume this is on the same hypervisor subnet - then most likely the reason you cannot connect from host Mac, is that you messed up somehow with networking... e.g. the port-forwarding at creation of instance, or the hosts file resolution, or similar.

At spin-up time, it can be a good idea to include the message-server standard port 3601 as part of the docker run or podman run command. Myself I always include it, but I suppose it is not part of the standard instructions... anyway the addition is:

-p 3601:3601

Unfortunately, without a lot of trickery we won't go into here, it's not easily possible to ensure this port-forwarding works later.

That is all the advice I can really give based on your issue description. Good luck with resolving the bug.

entry.title: SAP CPI HTTP Adapter not filtering data
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a scenario where I invoke a URL over HTTP and pass filter criteria.  Data set is like a set a cost centers and each cost center has some header fields and Attributes as a child node. In the Attributes a field by name "Active" is set to 1 for all valid records.  Objective is to get only records that are active that are set as 1.

Query: Attributes/Active=1

Issue: With this setting all cost center records are coming back and not getting filtered.  If an entry on the partner system is maintained without Attributes at all or without setting ACTIVE field they are being returned.  This is causing a issue since the process goes into a bad status.

Thanks in advance for any resolution.

SBapanapalli

entry.title: Re: How to work with a physical Tracking ID in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hello,

for a real solution that fits to the process I would need more Information.
Do you realy mean that you use a Data Colletion to map the first ID to the second ID or do you mean an assembly?
What is the cycle time a solution should fit to?
How many products per day?
Would it be possible to combine the IDs in one SFC?
For example ID1 = A101912 and ID2=B121341  SFC= B121341-A101912.
With the Work List API you could get every SFC and then filter it by a script task.

If there is no suitable API in the DMe, you could call a DMCi-API, to get the required information
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/insights/apis?locale=en-US

Otherwise may you could use the workinstruction APIs as workaround, if you don't want to call a DMCi-Rest-Service.
You could use a POST-Work Instruction API to create a WorkInstruction with the Name of the first ID and map it to the second.
For attachment you can unfortunately only use the order, not the SFC.
But you could write the SFC in a Work Instruction Element, use the description or something else.
Then you can always get the SFC or Order by using the GET-Work Instruction API.

Hope it helps you finding a solution.

Regards
Sascha

entry.title: Empowering Connections: Join Us at the SAP Business Women's Networking Lunch in Rotterdam 2025!
entry.content:
We are delighted to announce the return of SAP's Business Women's Networking Lunch for another year of inspiration, connection, and empowerment! Join us at the upcoming SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference in 2025 taking place in Rotterdam from April 8-10.

Save the date for our highly appreciated lunch on Wednesday, April 9 from 12:35 - 14:00. 

Together, we will celebrate the incredible impact of women in shaping the future of our industries. This year’s event promises to be an enriching experience filled with insightful conversations, meaningful connections, and inspiring stories of resilience and success.
Connect with like-minded professionals who are passionate about embracing diversity and harnessing the collective strength of a united community.

Here are some impressions of last year's event:

This year, we are happy to welcome two exceptional speakers who will share their insights and personal journeys:

  • Sarah Zeljko, Chair of Energy Queensland and leader in the energy industry, will discuss the challenges and opportunities of women in leadership.
  • Ann Daniels, an inspiring polar explorer that has worked with NASA and the European Space Agency, will take us on a journey of resilience, ambition, teamwork, and breaking barriers.

While this networking lunch is intended for women, we welcome everyone who shares the same passion for embracing diversity and the collective strength of individuals coming together.

Join us as we celebrate the powerful impact of women in shaping the future of the energy and utilities industries. We look forward to meeting new faces and reconnecting with familiar ones!

More details on how to participate will be shared as soon as you are registered for the conference itself. Let’s come together and make 2025 another memorable year for empowerment and connection!

entry.title: How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process
entry.content:
Introduction

Are you looking for a way to have responsive UI that starts an asynchronous process? 

With the background processing framework (BGPF) there is a way to start an asynchronous process from withing the RAP framework. This also allows to start processes that perform a commit work.

In this blog post I will show an easy way to start an asynchronous process using BGPF (the calculation of an inventory entry) and an easy way how to update the UI with the result of the calculation since with the latest release of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment a new feature was released which allows to refresh the UI of a SAP Fiori Elements applications by simply raising an event.   

 

 

 

Overview

A detailed introduction of the background processing can be found in the following blog post here Introducing the Background Processing Framework .

The background processing framework is the technology of choice when you want to start asynchronous processes in ABAP cloud rather than using application jobs which are meant to be used for long running and recurring processes. 

The generell problem when starting a long running process from within a stateless UI was that the UI could not be updated asynchronously unless you wanted to block the usage of the UI until the long running process has finished.

The source code of this sample class can be found here.   

 Event driven side effects now allow to update the UI of a Fiori Elements application when an asynchronous process has finished. 

When working on the implementation a colleage of mine, Dr. Martin Mueller, showed me an elegant way how a process can be started using BGPF by simply using a class that uses a class with a static method that takes the key of the updated entity of our RAP BO as a parameter in the save sequence of our RAP busines object.

 

 

DATA(bgpf_process_name) = zbgpfcl_calc_inventory_006=>run_via_bgpf( i_rap_bo_entity_key = update_inventory-%key ).

 

 

Implementation details

You can find the source code of a simple sample application in the following GitHub repository.

SAP-samples - How to use BGPF and events for side effects in ABAP RESTful programming model 

Further information

Event Drive Side Effects

  • SAP Online Help

Background Processing Framework

  • Blog Post: Introducing the Background Processing Framework 
  • SAP Online Help The RAP Transactional Model and the SAP LUW

entry.title: Re: Text-to-speech in simulations: change voice in SEN
entry.content:
Hi Laura,

Enable Now supports three types of Text to Speech languages - the standard Microsoft Windows languages, Google Text to Speech, and Microsoft Azure Text to Speech.  If any of these systems support Flemish then you will be able to generate audio using the corresponding voice.

I know that there is a Windows Language Pack for Flemish but I'm not sure if it supports TTS.  Google and Azure also appear to support Dutch - Belgium voices so one of those might provide a good result.

There is an information session running on European time on 19th March on the topic of audio, including the use of Text to Speech, in Enable Now.  You can access the registration page for the session using this link.

Regards
Shane

And lastly, these are the two current SAP Build certifications:

  • SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build 
  • SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration 

Let me know if you have any questions!

entry.title: HCM Brasil – Nota Técnica S-1.3 Nº 03/2025
entry.content:
Olá Pessoal, 

Hoje, dia 25 de fevereiro de 2025, concluímos a entrega da primeira fase da Nota Técnica S-1.3 Nº 03/2025, que permite o envio do número do contrato do eConsignado com 15 posições e possibilita que empresas do Setor Público adiram à Publicação da Instrução Normativa RFB Nº 2.239/2024. 

Os eventos que contemplam esta atualização são:  

  • S-1000 - Informações do empregador/contribuinte  
  • S-1200 - Remuneração de trabalhador vinculado ao Regime Geral de Previdência Social  
  • S-2299 - Desligamento  
  • S-2399 - Trabalhador sem vínculo de emprego – término 
  • S-1202 - Remuneração de Servidor vinculado ao Regime Próprio de Previdência Social 
  • S-1207 - Benefícios - Entes Públicos  
  • S-5003 - Informações do FGTS por trabalhador  

A Nota Técnica inclui as seguintes SAP Notes disponibilizadas: 

  • 3570837 - Prerequisite DDIC objects for SAP Note 3570835 
  • 3570838 - Prerequisite transformation object for SAP Note 3570835 
  • 3570835 - eSocial S-1.3 - NT 03/2025 - S-1000 - New field indPertIRRF 
  • 3570845 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3570841 
  • 3570841 - eSocial S-1.3 - NT 03/2025 - Payroll events - Change in field nrDoc 
  • 3571224 - eSocial S-1.3 - NT 03/2025 - S-5003 - Field nrContrato 

 

Empresas do Setor Público:  

Para aderir à Publicação da Instrução Normativa RFB Nº 2.239/2024 e informar ao governo o Indicador de pertencimento do IRRF no evento S-1000, é necessário seguir as orientações conforme mencionado na SAP Note 3570835.  

 

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você consegue encontrar na SAP Note 3556989 - eSocial - Overview - 2025.  

 

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.  

Fique à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este post no espaço abaixo. Lembre-se de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para acompanhar as últimas notícias.  

 

Atenciosamente,

Guilherme Zenzen

entry.title: Conditional formula to loop through a field and count replicates of records
entry.content:
I am looking for a Conditional formula to loop through a field and count replicates of records which do not have the status of cancelled (i.e. STATUS <> 'X')

I have a normal row i.e. Details a below in the picture if the record replicate = 1, and a cross tab table if the replicate > 1. Based on the count I need to suppress the normal row or the crosstab. So i want to create a formula which checks the number of replicates  using the field F9Results.REPLICATE_COUNT. 

I created the following formula (ctNotXRep) for the above.

WhilePrintingRecords;
NumberVar total;
Numbervar recNo;

if OnFirstRecord OR {F9Results.REPLICATE_COUNT} <> Previous({F9Results.REPLICATE_COUNT}) then 
    (
    total := 0;
    recNo := 1;
    );

//Add to the total if we're on a "good" record
if ({F9Results.STATUS} <> 'X') THEN
If recNo <= {F9Results.REPLICATE_COUNT} then (
  total := total + 1;
  recNo := recNo + 1;
) else (
  total := total;
  recNo := recNo + 1;
);
""

Then I created another formula sumNotXrep and placed it in the Report Footer. 

WhilePrintingRecords;
NumberVar total

An example data:

In the above example: the counts to be returned are count(Pen) = 5, count(RTFO) = 1 - since the cancelled row should not be counted. 

I am not getting what I want. Where am I going wrong?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 - Weiterführung nach Testphase & ABAP-Entwicklung
entry.content:
Hallo zusammen,

wir haben die 30-Tage-Testversion "SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance" genutzt und diese hat für unser Startup Unternehmen erfolgreich funktioniert. Nun möchten wir die Umgebung kostenpflichtig weiterführen.

Soweit ich verstanden habe, ist hierfür eine gültige S/4HANA-Lizenz erforderlich.
Ich gehe davon aus, dass folgende Lizenz für uns passend wäre:
Cloud Test, Demo, and Development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, one-system landscape (8012331) – 450,00 EUR

Meine Fragen dazu:

  1. Sobald wir diese Lizenz erworben haben, können wir dann unsere bestehende SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL)-Instanz weiterführen oder muss eine neue Instanz aufgesetzt werden?
  2. Aktuell erhalten wir erwerben einer SAP CAL Abonnent folgende Fehlermeldung – ist das normal, solange keine gültige Lizenz hinterlegt ist?
  3. Können wir auf der "SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance"-Instanz eigene ABAP-Programme entwickeln und einen Namensraum reservieren?

Vielen Dank im Voraus für eure Unterstützung!

Beste Grüße
zero4788

--

Hello everyone,

We have been using the "SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance" 30-day trial, and it has worked successfully for our startup. Now, we would like to continue using it on a paid basis.

As I understand, a valid S/4HANA license is required for this. I assume the following license would be the right choice for us:
Cloud Test, Demo, and Development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, one-system landscape (8012331) – 450.00 EUR

My questions regarding this:

  1. Once we purchase this license, can we continue using our existing SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL) instance, or do we need to set up a new one?
  2. We are currently receiving an error message when to buy CAL abbonenent– is this normal as long as there is no valid license assigned?


  3. Can we develop our own ABAP programs and reserve a namespace on the "SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance" instance?

Thanks in advance for your support!

Best regards,
zero4788

Hi @kumar_semmalai ,

it works without the Guided Process Solution, but the best solution depends on your needs.
I only wanted to make sure that no Assembly or Data Collection could be done without having started the selected SFC.

Therefore, I configured the Work Center POD as described below:
1. Open the Configuration of the responsible Navigation Button

2. Click on "Assign Actions"

3. Add a Production Process which Checks the SFC-Status

4. Maintain the required SFC-Status

The Production Process:

Hope it helps you.

entry.title: Re: Implementing FIFO Tracking in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hi @MuratSerhatYuksel,

to give you an exact solution I would need more Information.
What is the detailed process? When do you split the billet and how do you do this?

But my first suggestion would be to check the "Manage Next Numbers" App.
There you can define Next Numbers for the SFC Serialize functionality

For example you could do it like shown below.
The Timestamp in the Prefix will ensure that it is FIFO, if the time of Split is the right criteria.
The SFC will give you the overview which SFC it comes out of.


Hope it helps you.

Regards,
Sascha
